document.heading: Zoning – Uses – Smoke Shop
An Ordinance Concerning Zoning – Uses – Smoke Shop

para.heading: Roofline.
para.text: "Roofline" means the top edge of a roof or building parapet, whichever is higher, excluding any cupolas, pylons, chimneys, or minor projections.

para.heading: "Rooming unit" defined.
para.text: In this section, "rooming unit" means any room or group of rooms that forms a single habitable unit occupied or designed or intended to be occupied for sleeping or living purposes, but not for cooking purposes.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Rooming house" means a building that:
is not a dwelling;

contains 3 or more rooming units occupied or designed or intended to be occupied as a primary residence by individuals who, even though they do share common areas and facilities, do not form a single housekeeping unit and do not provide compensation under a single lease for occupancy of the facility; and
provides shared facilities such as a kitchen and bath.

para.heading: Rowhouse.
para.text: See "Dwelling: Rowhouse".

para.heading: Satellite dish antenna.
para.text: "Satellite dish antenna" means a dish antenna for transmitting signals to a receiver or receiving station or for receiving television, radio, data, communication, or other signals from satellites or from other antennas or services.

para.heading: Seasonal or holiday sales lot.
para.text: "Seasonal or holiday sales lot" means a temporary retail sales operation, generally conducted outdoors, that offers for sale seasonal or holiday related items, such as Christmas trees and pumpkins.

para.heading: Semi-detached dwelling.
para.text: See "Dwelling: Semi-detached".

para.heading: Shared-use path.
para.text: "Shared-use path" means a dedicated way that:
has a paved or hard surface;
is designed for individual travel or recreational use;
accommodates walking, running, biking, use of a mobility device, scooter, or other recreational vehicle; and
is not for vehicular traffic

para.heading: Shielded lighting.
para.text: See "Lighting: Shielded".

para.heading: Shipyard.
para.text: "Shipyard" means any facility or area that is designed or used for the manufacture, assembly, or repair of ships, barges, or boats.

para.heading: Side lot line.
para.text: See "Lot line: Side".

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Sign" means any writing (including letter, word, or numeral), pictorial representation (including illustration or graphic), emblem (including logo, symbol, or trademark), device, or any other figure of similar character that:

is attached to, painted on, projected on, or in any other way represented on a structure, in or on a window (including anything affixed to the interior and displayed within 12 inches of a window), or on the ground;
is used to announce, direct attention, or advertise; and
is visible from outside a structure.

para.heading: Types of signs.
para.text: Specific types of signs are as described in § 17-102 {"Signs: Definitions"} of this Code.

para.heading: Significant development.
para.text: "Significant development", as it applies to the Chesapeake Bay Critical Area, has the meaning stated in § 7-402 {"CBCA Overlay: Definitions"} of this Code.
“Smoke shop” means a retail goods establishment that:

is engaged in the retail sale or on-site consumption of tobacco products, tobacco smoking accessories, any device or paraphernalia that can be used to deliver nicotine or other substances to the individual inhaling from the device, or any cartridge, component, or accessory of the device; and
operates under 1 or more of the following licenses established by the State Business Regulation Article:
other tobacco products (OTP) retailer, as established by § 16.5-201 {“Licenses Required Relating to Tobacco Products”};
electronic smoking devices retailer, as established by § 16.7-201 {“License Required”};
vape shop vendor, as established by § 16.7-201 {“License Required”};
a cigarette retailer, as established by Subtitle 2 of Title 16 {“Cigarette Business Licenses”}; or
a tobacconist license, as established by § 16.5-201 {“Licenses Required Relating to Tobacco Products”} of the State Business Regulation Article; and
devotes more than 10 linear feet of display area, measured by shelf space, counter space, and wall-mounted displays, whether vertical or horizontal, to:
other tobacco products, as defined by § 16.5-101(j) of the State Business Regulation Article;
electronic smoking devices, as defined by § 16.7-101(c) of the State Business Regulation Article; or
a combination of both tobacco products and electronic smoking devices.

para.heading: Exclusions.
para.text: “Smoke shop” does not include a retail goods establishment:
that primarily derives its revenue from the sale of premium cigars, pipe tobacco, membership fees, or locker rentals; or
where the sale of tobacco products is secondary to the establishment’s primary retail function and less than 25% of the establishment’s revenue is derived from the sale of tobacco products.

para.heading: Social club.
para.text: See "Lodge or social club".

para.heading: Solid waste sanitary landfill.
para.text: See "Landfill: Solid waste sanitary".

para.heading: Special exception.
para.text: See "Use: Conditional".

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Stadium" means a structure with tiers of seats rising around a field or court, intended to be used:
primarily for the viewing of athletic events; and
secondarily, for entertainment and other public gathering purposes, such as conventions, circuses, or concerts.

para.heading: Inclusions.
para.text: "Stadium" includes the following accessory uses designed and intended primarily for patrons of the facility:
a gift or souvenir shop; and
a restaurant or refreshment stands.

para.heading: Stacking space.
para.text: "Stacking space" means a space specifically designated as a waiting area for vehicles patronizing a drive-through establishment.

para.heading: Stormwater.
para.text: "Stormwater" means the water running off the surface of a drainage area during and immediately following rain or as a result of other precipitation.

para.heading: Story.
para.text: "Story" means that portion of a building, other than a basement, that is:
included between the surface of any floor and the surface of the floor next above it; or
if there is no floor above it, then the space between the floor and the ceiling next above it.

para.heading: Structural alteration.
para.text: "Structural alteration" means:
a change in the permanent physical members of a structure, such as bearing walls, columns, beams, or girders; or
any substantial change in the roof or in the exterior walls.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Structure" means all or any part of anything erected that:
has a fixed location on the ground; or
is attached to something having a fixed location on the ground.

para.heading: Inclusions.
para.text: "Structure" includes any building, fence, wall, sign, or tower.

para.heading: Structure: Accessory.
para.text: See "Accessory structure".

para.heading: Substantial construction.
para.text: "Substantial construction" means:
for new construction, that construction materials have been placed in permanent positions or fastened in a permanent manner; or
in preparation for rebuilding, that excavation, demolition, or removal of an existing structure has been substantially begun and is carried on diligently.

section.heading: Smoke shops.
section.text: A smoke shop may not be located:

para.text: within 750 feet of any school, recreation center, or park; or
within 750 feet from another smoke shop.

para.heading: License required.
para.text: A tavern must have the appropriate license from the Baltimore City Board of Liquor License Commissioners.

para.heading: Sales for off-premises consumption.
para.text: Taverns may sell alcoholic beverages for off-premises consumption only if:

annually, the average daily receipts from the sale of alcoholic beverages for on-premises consumption exceeds 50% of the establishment's total average daily receipts, not including sales of novelty items, income from vending machines, cover charges, or other receipts not derived from the sale of food or beverages; and
more than 50% of the establishment's public floor space is devoted to on-premises consumption.

para.heading: Records.
para.text: Taverns must keep accurate records of all sales of alcoholic beverages so that a determination can be made of what proportion of those sales are in sealed packages or containers for off-premise consumption. These records must be maintained and made available for inspection to confirm compliance.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: In this section, the following terms have the meanings indicated.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Base station" means the transmission equipment and non-tower supporting structure at a fixed location that enables wireless communications services between user equipment and a communications network.

para.heading: Exclusions.
para.text: "Base station" does not include a satellite dish antenna, as defined in § 1-313(d) {"Satellite dish antenna"} of this Code.

para.heading: Collocate.
para.text: "Collocate" means to mount or install transmission equipment on telecommunication facilities for the purpose of transmitting or receiving radio frequency signals for communications purposes.

para.heading: Distributed antenna system; DAS.
para.text: "Distributed antenna system" or "DAS" means a network of multiple, spatially separate antenna nodes connected, for the purpose of providing wireless communication services within a geographic area.

para.heading: Eligible facilities request.
para.text: "Eligible facilities request" means any request for modification of an existing tower or base station that:
involves:
the collocation of new transmission equipment;
the removal of transmission equipment; or
the replacement of transmission equipment; and
does not:
substantially change physical dimensions; or
include the replacement of a tower.

para.heading: Node.
para.text: "Node" means a component of a DAS or small cell installation that:
includes one or more radio frequency transmitters or antennae; and
is connected by a high capacity transport medium (such as a fiber optic cable) to a common source with other nodes.

para.heading: Small cell installation.
para.text: "Small cell installation" means a wireless communication technology installation similar to a DAS, but normally including a single node.

para.heading: Substantially change ... physical dimensions.
para.text: "Substantially change ... physical dimensions", when used with reference to a tower or base station, means:
for a tower:
an increase in the tower's height by more than 10% or by the height of one additional antenna array with separation from the nearest existing antenna not to exceed 20 feet, whichever is greater; or
the creation of an appurtenance that protrudes from the edge of the tower more than 20 feet or more than the width of the tower structure at the level of the appurtenance, whichever is greater;
for a base station:
an increase in the base station's height by more than 10% or 10 feet, whichever is greater; or
the creation of an appurtenance that protrudes from the edge of the base station by more than 6 feet;
an installation of more than the standard number of new equipment cabinets for the technology involved or, in any event, more than 4 cabinets;
a modification that entails an excavation or deployment outside the current site of the tower or base station;
a modification that would defeat the existing concealment elements of the tower or base station; and

a modification that does not comply with conditions associated with the prior approval of construction or modification of the tower or base station, unless the non-compliance is due to an increase in height, increase in width, additional cabinet installation, or new excavation that does not exceed the corresponding "substantial change" thresholds identified above.

para.heading: Telecommunications facility.
para.text: "Telecommunications facility" means any structure that falls within the definition of "base station" or "tower".

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Tower" means any structure built for the sole or primary purpose of supporting a base station.

para.heading: Exclusions.
para.text: "Tower" does not include a satellite dish antenna, as defined in § 1-313(d) {"Satellite dish antenna"} of this Code.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Transmission equipment" means any equipment that facilitates transmission for a wireless communication service.

para.heading: Inclusions.
para.text: "Transmission equipment" includes radio transceivers, antennas, and other relevant equipment associated with and necessary to their operation, including coaxial or fiber-optic cable, and regular and backup power supply.

para.heading: Exclusions.
para.text: "Transmission equipment" does not include a satellite dish antenna, as defined in § 1-313(d) {"Satellite dish antenna"} of this Code.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Wireless communications service" means a radiocommunication service licensed pursuant to Federal Communications Commission requirements for frequency bands in the 2305-2320 and 2345-2360 MHZ spectrum range.

para.heading: Illustrations.
para.text: The most common use of this spectrum is mobile voice and data services, including cell phone, text messaging, and Internet.

para.heading: Exclusions from section.
para.text: This section does not apply to towers or base stations located in a public right-of-way.

para.heading: Purpose of standards.
para.text: The following standards for telecommunications facilities are intended to:
ensure public health, safety, and welfare;
ensure access to reliable wireless communications services throughout the City;
encourage the location of antennas and towers on City-owned property;
encourage the use of existing telecommunications facilities for wireless communications services; and
encourage the location of towers, to the extent possible, in areas where the adverse impact on the City will be minimal and, preferably, in non-residential districts.

para.heading: Use approvals.
para.text: A telecommunications facility is, in all districts, a conditional use requiring approval by the Board of Municipal and Zoning Appeals, except where, as provided in subsection (k) {"Stealth design"} or subsection (n) {"Modifications"} of this section, it is a permitted use subject to site plan review.

para.heading: Application requirements.
para.text: In addition to the general requirements for conditional-use applications, site plan review submissions, eligible facilities requests, or other documentation, all applications to erect, construct, or modify any part of a telecommunications facility must include the following items:
a site plan showing:
the location, size, screening, and design of all structures, including fences;
the location and size of all outdoor equipment;
elevations showing antenna height;
a landscape plan; and
if the site plan is for a new tower, indication of the fall zone (shaded circle);

a maintenance plan, and any applicable maintenance agreement, designed to ensure long-term, continuous maintenance to a reasonably prudent standard, including maintenance of landscaping, keeping the area free from debris and litter, and immediate removal of any graffiti;
a description of what is proposed, demonstrating the need for the telecommunications facility to be located where proposed;
the reason or purpose for the placement, construction, or modification, with specific reference to the provider's coverage, capacity, and quality needs, goals, and objectives;
the service area of the proposed telecommunications facility;

if the proposal is for a new tower, a map showing existing telecommunications facilities within the City and within areas surrounding the borders of the City, together with a justification for why locating on those existing facilities is not feasible and the new tower needed where proposed;

certification by a professional engineer of the manner in which the proposed structure will fall, which certification may be used, along with other criteria such as applicable regulations for the district in question, in determining if additional setback should be required for the structure and other facilities; and

a visual simulation or rendering of the proposed telecommunications facility that illustrates the relationship between the height and the visual appearance of the telecommunications facility. The Zoning Administrator may require that the visual simulation be provided from 2 different perspectives and accurately depict the scale of the proposed telecommunications facility in the context of the surrounding area.
Telecommunications facilities must be set back from all lot lines in accordance with the minimum setback requirements applicable to the district in question.
DAS and small cell installation sites are not subject to setback requirements.

para.heading: Height.
para.text: The maximum height of a tower is the minimum needed to function satisfactorily with telecommunications facilities for at least 2 wireless communications service providers. The conditional-use application for approval of a tower must demonstrate the height needed for the tower.

para.heading: Lighting and marking.
para.text: Telecommunications facilities may not be lit or marked unless required by the Federal Communications Commission, by the Federal Aviation Administration, or for the specific purpose of security, maintenance, or repair.

para.heading: Landscaping.
para.text: Telecommunications facilities must comply with the landscaping regulations of the Baltimore City Landscape Manual.

A base station may house only equipment and supplies for the direct operation of the telecommunications facility. Equipment and supplies not used in direct support of the operation may not be stored on the site.
The facility may not be staffed.
Signs are strictly prohibited unless required by FCC or other governmental regulation.
A tower may be freestanding or supported and may be of either lattice or monopole construction.

The ability for other wireless communications service providers to collocate on a tower is required. Towers must be designed to accommodate other wireless communications service providers. The area surrounding a tower must be of a sufficient size to accommodate the base stations of at least 2 wireless communications service providers.
Unless otherwise required by the Federal Communications Commission, the Federal Aviation Administration, or the City, towers must have a galvanized silver or gray finish.
Stealth designs for base stations are encouraged and are permitted uses in all commercial, industrial, and special purpose districts, subject to site plan review and the requirements of this section.
An application for site plan review must include all information required by this section.
To qualify as a permitted stealth design:
the base station must be within an enclosed structure or otherwise screened from public view in accordance with the requirements of the Baltimore City Landscape Manual;

its antennae must be mounted at least 40 feet above grade, except for small cell installations, which must be mounted at least 25 feet above grade or a minimum of 15 feet by conditional use, as measured from grade to the base of the antenna;

the base station must be located on or in a structure already permitted within the zoning district, such as water towers, clock towers, streetlights, penthouses, parapet walls, and steeples, and designed to blend into the structure; and
the base station may not increase the overall height of the structure on which it is mounted.
Base stations that collocate on existing towers are considered stealth design.
Any telecommunications facility that is not operated for 180 consecutive days is considered abandoned.
The owner must remove the telecommunications facility and all aboveground equipment and related debris within 180 days of its abandonment.

Ordinary maintenance may be performed on nonconforming telecommunications facilities. However, if a proposed alteration intensifies a nonconforming characteristic of the telecommunications facility, a variance is required.

Collocation of a base station on an existing nonconforming tower is allowed as a conditional use requiring approval by the Board of Municipal and Zoning Appeals, as long as the addition of the base station does not intensify the nonconformity.

Modifications of an existing tower or base station that does not substantially change the physical dimensions of the tower or base station are permitted if supported by an eligible facilities request.
An applicant submitting an eligible facilities request must show that the modification does not substantially change the physical dimensions of the tower or base station.
Within 60 days of filing an eligible facilities request, the request must be reviewed and approved if the modification qualifies under this subsection,
Approval of an eligible facilities request:
is subject to a site plan review; and
may be subject to conditions imposed by the Zoning Administrator, as long as those conditions do not conflict with the provisions of this section.

para.heading: Certifications.
para.text: When the installation of a telecommunications facility is completed, and every 5th year thereafter, a professional engineer must certify that the facility meets all applicable Building Code and safety requirements.

para.heading: Management plan for certain activities.
para.text: Urban agriculture uses that involve any of the following activities must prepare a management plan, subject to approval by the Director of Planning, that addresses how the activities will be managed to mitigate impacts on surrounding land uses and natural systems:
Animal husbandry, including chicken coops, apiaries and aquaculture. The keeping of livestock must adhere to all Baltimore City Health Department and Maryland Department of Agriculture regulations.
Processing of food produced on site.
Spreading of manure, sludge, or other nutrient-rich fertilizers.
Spraying of agricultural chemicals, including fertilizers, fungicides, and pesticides.
Use of heavy equipment such as tractors.
Greenhouses (permanent or temporary) used to extend the growing season are permitted.
There is no limit on the number or square footage on these structures.

para.heading: Plants for human consumption.
para.text: For any urban agriculture use that involves the cultivation of plants for human consumption, measures must be taken to test and, if necessary, remediate the soil in accordance with guidelines adopted by the Department of Planning.
Permanent accessory structures are limited to:
tool sheds;
shade pavilions;
barns;
toilet facilities;
planting preparation houses; and
post-harvest processing facilities.
All structures must be set back at least 5 feet from any lot line.
No structure may exceed 25 feet in height, except for structures designed to capture wind energy.
The combined area of all structures is limited to 25% of the lot area.

For multiple adjoining lots that are under common ownership and used as community-managed open space, the limit for the combined area of structures is applied over the entire site rather than each individual lot. The limit for the combined area of structures for the individual lots may not exceed that of the underlying zoning district.
Farmstands for the display and sale of agricultural products are permitted.
Farmstands must be removed from the premises or stored inside a structure on the premises during that time of the year when the facility is not open for public use.

para.heading: Composting.
para.text: Composting on-site is allowed as an accessory use, subject to the following conditions:
any compost pile must be located at least 3 feet away from any lot line;
composting areas and structures must be maintained in a way that protects adjacent properties from nuisance odors and the attraction of rodents or other pests; and
organic waste material for composting may be accepted from outside sources and either used on site or distributed at no cost, but may not be sold.

para.heading: Effect on traffic, adjacent properties, etc.
para.text: Utilities and electric substations must be designed so that the location of entrances and exits, exterior lighting, service areas, and parking and loading facilities will minimize traffic congestion, pedestrian hazards, and adverse impacts on adjacent properties.

para.heading: Aboveground structures.
para.text: Except as otherwise provided in subsection (c) of this section, electric substations and any aboveground utility structures, such as pedestals for cable wire access or other access points for underground infrastructure (communications wiring, fiber optic, etc.):
may not encroach into a required front yard; and
must be screened from any public right-of-way.

para.heading: Exemptions from setback and screening requirements.
para.text: The following are exempt from the setback and screening requirements of subsection (b) of this section:
an above-ground, fully-enclosed transformer, switchgear, regulator, meter, or capacitor that is located in an Industrial Zoning District; and
an above-ground, fully-enclosed transformer, switchgear, regulator, meter, or capacitor that:
is located in a Residential, Office-Residential, Commercial, or TOD Zoning District; and
does not exceed 7 feet in width, 7 feet in length, or 6 feet in height.

para.heading: Modifications to electric substations.
para.text: On a property where an electric substation has been approved as a conditional use, a modification of the electric substation is allowed without amendment of the conditional use, as long as:
the modification conforms to the bulk and yard regulations of the underlying zoning district;
the modification either:
is located solely within the existing perimeter fence or wall; or
covers an area beyond the existing perimeter fence or wall that is not more than 10% of the area within the existing perimeter fence or wall;
the modification is located solely on the property governed by the conditional use; and

the electric substation, as so modified, complies with all conditions of the existing conditional use approval other than a condition that restricts the electric substation to the equipment configuration allowed under the existing conditional use approval.

section.heading: Measurement of distance between properties.
section.text: In determining the distance between properties, the measurement shall be calculated using the shortest separation between the property boundary lines.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: Except as provided in subsection (b) of this section, smoke shops located within 750 feet of any school, recreation center, or park shall be terminated no later than 2 years after the date on which the use became nonconforming.

para.heading: Board authority.
para.text: The Board of Municipal and Zoning Appeals may extend by an additional 1 year the time by which a smoke shop must terminate the nonconforming use.

para.heading: Timely application.
para.text: To obtain an extension, the property owner or lessee shall apply to the Board, in writing, within 1 year after the property becomes nonconforming.

para.heading: Factors for extension.
para.text: The property owner or lessee seeking an extension under paragraph (2) of this subsection shall establish the existence of 1 of the following factors that would render termination within the time required by subsection (a) of this section a hardship:
purchase of the property within the 3 years immediately preceding the nonconformity;
investment in capital improvements to the property in excess of $100,000 before June 30, 2025, and after April 1, 2021; or
a lease that was entered into before April 1, 2021, has a term remaining in excess of 10 years, and is not terminable by the lessee because of zoning changes prohibiting the use.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: Nonconforming taverns must either:
fully comply with § 14-338 {"Taverns"} within 2 years after they become nonconforming; or
be terminated.

para.heading: Waiver for hardship.
para.text: A waiver extending the time for compliance based on hardship may be applied for in accordance with § 18-701(b) {"Retail good establishments...: Waiver for hardship"} of this subtitle.

attachment.heading: Open-Space Districts – Permitted and Conditional Uses

tr.td: 1 Only Wireless Communication Services that are modifications to – and do not substantially change the physical dimension of – an existing telecommunications facility, are considered permitted uses.

attachment.heading: Detached and Semi-Detached Residential Districts – Permitted and Conditional Uses

tr.td: Electric Substation: Enclosed, Indoor, or Outdoor

td.p: 1 A Community-Based Alternative Energy System: (i) if on a lot less than 0.5 acre in lot area, requires conditional-use approval by the Zoning Board, and (ii) if on a lot 0.5 acres or more in lot area, is a permitted use.
2 Only Wireless Communication Services that are modifications to – and do not substantially change the physical dimension of – an existing telecommunications facility, are considered permitted uses.

attachment.heading: Rowhouse and Multi-Family Residential Districts – Permitted and Conditional Uses

tr.td: Electric Substation: Enclosed, Indoor, or Outdoor

td.p: 1 A Community-Based Alternative Energy System: (i) if on a lot less than 0.5 acre in lot area, requires conditional-use approval by the Zoning Board, and (ii) if on a lot 0.5 acres or more in lot area, is a permitted use.
2 Only Wireless Communication Services that are modifications to – and do not substantially change the physical dimension of – an existing telecommunications facility, are considered permitted uses.

attachment.heading: Commercial Districts – Permitted and Conditional Uses

tr.td: Entertainment: Live (Accessory to Restaurant, Tavern, Art Studio, or Art Gallery)
Motor Vehicle Rental Establishment (Fully Enclosed Structure)
Motor Vehicle Rental Establishment (Outdoor Vehicle Display)
Motor Vehicle Service and Repair: Major
Motor Vehicle Service and Repair: Minor (Fully Enclosed Structure)
Motor Vehicle Service and Repair: Minor (Outdoor Vehicle Storage)
Retail Goods Establishment (No Alcoholic Beverages Sales)
Retail Goods Establishment (With Alcoholic Beverages Sales)

td.p: 1 Allowed only in a structure lawfully existing as of June 5, 2017.
2 However, along Pratt Street in the C-5 District, principal-use parking garages are prohibited. See § 10-503(c)(1).
3 Only telecommunications base stations that comply with the stealth design standards of § 14-339 are considered permitted uses.
4 Only Wireless Communication Services that are modifications to – and do not substantially change the physical dimension of – an existing telecommunications facility, are considered permitted uses.

5 Under § 14-329(a)(1) and (a)(2), both outdoor dining and rooftop dining, respectively, require approval as a conditional use by the Board of Municipal and Zoning Appeals if located in a C-1, C-1-E, C-1-VC, or C-2 zoning district .

attachment.heading: Industrial Districts – Permitted and Conditional Uses

tr.td: Entertainment: Live (Accessory to Restaurant, Tavern, Art Studio, or Art Gallery)
Motor Vehicle Service and Repair: Major
Motor Vehicle Service and Repair: Minor
Retail Goods Establishment (No Alcoholic Beverages Sales)
Retail Goods Establishment (With Alcoholic Beverages Sales)

td.p: 1 Allowed only when (i) accessory to an office structure, research and development facility, or industrial use, and (ii) integrated into that structure, facility, or use to serve its employees.
2 Allowed only when secondary to a primary industrial use.
3 Office uses legally established as of the effective date of this Code are deemed conforming and are not required to be secondary to a primary industrial use.
4 Only telecommunications base stations that comply with the stealth design standards of § 14-339 are considered permitted uses.
5 Only Wireless Communication Services that are modifications to – and do not substantially change the physical dimension of – an existing telecommunications facility, are considered permitted uses.

attachment.heading: Office-Residential Districts – Permitted and Conditional Uses

tr.td: Day Care Centers: Adult or Child
Retail Goods Establishment (No Alcoholic Beverages Sales)
Electric Substation: Enclosed, Indoor, or Outdoor

td.p: 1 These are permitted uses only if: (i) the structure has 50 or more dwelling units; and (ii) non-residential uses are limited to 10% of the structure's gross floor area.
2 Only telecommunications base stations that comply with the stealth design standards of § 14-339 are considered permitted uses.
3 Only Wireless Communication Services that are modifications to – and do not substantially change the physical dimension of – an existing telecommunications facility, are considered permitted uses.

attachment.heading: Transit-Oriented Development Districts – Permitted and Conditional Uses

tr.td: Retail Goods Establishment (No Alcoholic Beverages Sales)
Retail Goods Establishment (With Alcoholic Beverages Sales)
Electric Substation: Enclosed, Indoor, or Outdoor

td.p: 1 No recreation facilities are allowed, except for standard playground equipment and outdoor furniture.
2 Not to exceed 5,000 sq. ft. of the structure's gross floor area.
3 Only telecommunications base stations that comply with the stealth design standards of § 14-339 are considered permitted uses.
4 Only Wireless Communication Services that are modifications to – and do not substantially change the physical dimension of – an existing telecommunications facility, are considered permitted uses.

attachment.heading: Educational Campus Districts – Permitted and Conditional Uses

td.p: Recreation: Indoor or Outdoor
(Ancillary to Educational Facility)

tr.td: Retail Goods Establishment (No Alcoholic Beverages Sales)
Electric Substation: Enclosed, Indoor, or Outdoor

td.p: 1 Only telecommunications base stations that comply with the stealth design standards of § 14-339 are considered permitted uses.
2 Only Wireless Communication Services that are modifications to – and do not substantially change the physical dimension of – an existing telecommunications facility, are considered permitted uses.

attachment.heading: Hospital Campus Districts – Permitted and Conditional Uses

tr.td: Dormitory (Ancillary to Hospital or Educational Facility: Post-Secondary)
Dwelling (Ancillary to Hospital or Educational Facility: Post-Secondary)
Community Center (Ancillary to Educational Facility: Post-Secondary)
Cultural Facility (Ancillary to Educational Facility: Post-Secondary)
Recreation: Indoor and Outdoor (Ancillary to Hospital or Educational Facility: Post-Secondary)

td.p: Retail Goods Establishment (No Alcoholic
Beverages Sales)

tr.td: Electric Substation: Enclosed, Indoor, or Outdoor

td.p: 1 Only telecommunications base stations that comply with the stealth design standards of § 14-339 are considered permitted uses.
2 Only Wireless Communication Services that are modifications to – and do not substantially change the physical dimension of – an existing telecommunications facility, are considered permitted uses.

attachment.heading: Port Covington District – Permitted and Conditional Uses

tr.td: Motor Vehicle Rental Establishment (Fully Enclosed Structure)
Motor Vehicle Rental Establishment (Outdoor Vehicle Display)
Motor Vehicle Service and Repair: Major
Motor Vehicle Service and Repair: Minor (Fully Enclosed Structure)
Motor Vehicle Service and Repair: Minor (Outdoor Vehicle Storage)
Retail Goods Establishment (No Alcoholic Beverages Sales)
Retail Goods Establishment (With Alcoholic Beverages Sales)
Electric Substation: Enclosed, Indoor, or Outdoor

td.p: 1 Subject to conformance with the Marina Master Plan.
2 Only telecommunications base stations that comply with the stealth design standards of § 14-339 are considered permitted uses.
3 Only Wireless Communication Services that are modifications to – and do not substantially change the physical dimension of – an existing telecommunications facility, are considered permitted uses.

attachment.heading: Harford Road Overlay District – Office, Commercial, and Industrial Districts – Permitted and Conditional Uses

tr.td: Retail Goods Establishment (No Alcoholic Beverages Sales)
Retail Goods Establishment (With Alcoholic Beverages Sales)
Electric Substation: Enclosed, Indoor, or Outdoor

td.p: 1 Only telecommunications base stations that comply with the stealth design standards of § 14-339 are considered permitted uses.
2 Only Wireless Communication Services that are modifications to – and do not substantially change the physical dimension of – an existing telecommunications facility, are considered permitted uses.
